A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent used a naive implementation (execute_python call function-call-4521043477466583737) that detected publication year by taking the first “20xx” in only the first 3000 characters and labeled “empirical” via simple substring search, which missed most qualifying papers and led to querying citations (query_db call function-call-7818898384745143981) for only two titles, producing an incomplete and incorrect result.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes

In the contributing execute_python step (function-call-1144066629538908940), the agent incorrectly implemented metadata extraction by treating any occurrence of the word "empirical" anywhere in the full text as an 'empirical' contribution and inferring publication year as the most frequent 20xx in the entire document, then summing citations from the Citations table, leading to misclassified papers and mismatched total citation counts.
